name: launch-day
description: dApp-launch congestion profile calibrated to the January 2022 SundaeSwap launch, using per-block chain data (Koios/db-sync, Dec 25 2021 - Feb 10 2022; see docs/phase-2/launch-day-daily-chain-load.csv). Measured daily byte fullness against the 72KB block cap: ~31% baseline (late Dec), ~60% build-up (Jan 5-14), ~88% pre-launch surge (Jan 15-19), ~93% plateau from launch Jan 20 through Jan 27+, never exceeding 94.4%. Stages map those levels onto the sim's EB byte cap (~485 tx/slot at mean tx size): 160 (~33%), 290 (~60%), 430 (~89%), 600 (~123%, assumed onset overshoot - fullness is capacity-pinned and cannot show offered demand above it), 450 (~93%, outlasting the run horizon as the real event outlasted two weeks). Urgency multipliers are modelling assumptions, not measurements: x2 during the pre-launch surge and plateau, x4 at onset (maps each sim urgency band onto roughly the next band up); value distribution unchanged. The profile replays the event's saturation fractions, not its absolute volumes: 2022 daily transaction counts (~195k/day peak) would not stress linear-Leios.
